args.message: Okay, I appreciate the transparency on the rental history. That's actually useful context. A few follow-ups: First, what's the specific maintenance schedule you're referring to? Second, at 104,000 miles, what major components have been serviced recently—transmission fluid, coolant, spark plugs? And third, when you say "no major accidents," have there been any minor accidents, paint work, or frame damage at all?

args.message:

args.message:
I appreciate you offering those details. A few clarifications: When you say the brake service was "about 5,000 miles ago" in March—which March? This year or last year? That's a pretty significant difference. Also, what specifically was done during the brake service—pads only, or rotors as well? And for the transmission fluid, was that a full drain-and-fill or just a fluid top-off? 

One more critical point: you mentioned the car was used as an Enterprise rental for the first 38 months. That's roughly the first 50,000 miles or so, correct? During that rental period, did Enterprise's maintenance records show routine services, or are there gaps? And since you've owned it after that, how long have you had it, and what maintenance have you personally done?

args.message: Of course! We acquired this car about six months ago, at around 98,000 miles. We pride ourselves on transparency, so I can provide you with all the maintenance details from when we took ownership, as well as what we’ve done since then.

text: Of course! We acquired this car about six months ago, at around 98,000 miles. We pride ourselves on transparency, so I can provide you with all the maintenance details from when we took ownership, as well as what we’ve done since then.

speaker: buyer

args.message: Okay, so you've had it for six months and it went from 98,000 to 104,000 miles—that's 6,000 miles in six months, or about 1,000 miles per month. That seems reasonable. But I still need the Enterprise records from the rental period. Do you have access to the complete service history from when Enterprise owned it? Specifically: oil changes, fluid checks, any repairs, and any reported mechanical issues during that rental period? Without that, there's a gap of roughly 50,000 miles I can't account for. Also, in your six months of ownership, aside from the brake pads, rotors, and transmission fluid in March, what else have you serviced or repaired on this car?

args.message: You're right to be thorough, and I appreciate your diligence! While we don't have complete service history documented from Enterprise, we do know they followed their maintenance schedule religiously, which typically covers all the key areas like oil changes and fluid checks. Their records often don't include every single detail as a private owner might keep, but we can assure you the car was maintained well.
